post.summary: Authenticate a user and obtain a JWT access token.
post.description: Behavior: Validates the user's credentials and, if successful, creates a new session and returns a JWT access token with a refresh token. Accounts that are disabled, expired, pending invitation, or pending approval will be rejected. Guest users must have a valid profile and permitted application access. Authorization: Public endpoint — no authentication required. Side Effects: Creates a new user session. The login event is recorded for auditing purposes. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is null. Returns 404 if authentication fails or the user cannot be found. Returns 403 if the user account is Disabled, Expired, PendingInvitation, or PendingApproval. LoginStatus on the response indicates the specific failure reason (e.g., IsDisabled, IsPendingEmailConfirmation, RequiresNewPassword).

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Security.LoginRequestModel containing the user's email (UserName), password, and optional ApplicationId for session tracking.

200.description: Authentication succeeded. Returns the JWT token, refresh token, user profile, and session information.

400.description: The request body is null or missing required fields (UserName, Password).

404.description: Authentication failed — invalid credentials or user not found.

post.summary: Log out the currently authenticated user and end their session.
post.description: Behavior: Ends the user's active session and invalidates the authentication token. If a UserSessionId is provided, that specific session is terminated. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token ([Authorize]). Side Effects: Terminates the user session. The logout event is recorded for auditing purposes. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Security.LogoutRequestModel containing the optional UserSessionId to delete and optional ApplicationId.

200.description: The user was successfully logged out and the session was deleted.

post.summary: Check if the user session is still valid and update the session activity timestamp.
post.description: Behavior: Validates that the specified session is still active and keeps it alive by updating its last activity timestamp. If single-session enforcement is enabled, detects whether the session has been replaced by a newer login. Also checks whether the user's account has expired. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token ([Authorize]). Side Effects: Updates the session's last activity timestamp. May mark the session as expired if the user account is no longer valid. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is null. Returns PingStatus.AccountExpired if the user or session is expired. Returns PingStatus.SessionReplaced if single-session enforcement detected a newer session.

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Account.PingRequestModel containing the UserSessionId and optional ApplicationId.

200.description: The session is valid. PingStatus will be Normal for active sessions, or AccountExpired/SessionReplaced if the session should be terminated.

400.description: The request body is null or UserSessionId is missing.

post.summary: Retrieve a new JWT access token using a refresh token.
post.description: Behavior: Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new JWT access token. The response includes the new token, a new refresh token, and the expiration duration in seconds. Authorization: Public endpoint — no authentication required. The refresh token itself serves as the credential. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is null. Returns 404 if the refresh token is invalid or expired.

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Account.RefreshTokenRequestModel containing the RefreshToken obtained from a previous login or token refresh.

200.description: Token refresh succeeded. Returns a new JWT token and refresh token.

400.description: The request body is null or RefreshToken is missing.

404.description: The refresh token is invalid or has expired.

post.summary: Change the email address of the currently authenticated user.
post.description: Behavior: Updates the authenticated user's email address to the new value provided. The current password must be supplied for verification. After a successful change, the user must re-authenticate with the new email address. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token ([Authorize]). Side Effects: Changes the user's email address. The user must re-authenticate after the change. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request is null or the authenticated user's email cannot be determined. Throws if the user account is disabled.

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Account.ChangeEmailRequestModel containing the current Password for verification and the NewEmail to set.

200.description: The email was successfully changed. The user must re-authenticate with the new email.

400.description: The request is null, the CognitoEmail claim is missing, or the user account is disabled.

post.summary: Change the password of the currently authenticated user.
post.description: Behavior: Updates the authenticated user's password. The current password must be supplied for verification along with the new password. The existing access token remains valid until expiration. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token ([Authorize]). Side Effects: Updates the user's password. The existing token remains valid until expiration. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request is null or the authenticated user's email cannot be determined. Throws if the user account is disabled or the current password is incorrect.

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Account.ChangePasswordRequestModel containing the current Password for verification and the NewPassword to set.

200.description: The password was successfully changed.

400.description: The request is null, the CognitoEmail claim is missing, the current password is incorrect, or the user is disabled.

post.summary: Trigger a password reset code to be sent to the user's email address.
post.description: Behavior: Initiates the password reset flow for the specified user account. If the account is active, a 6-digit verification code is sent to the user's email address. The code should then be submitted to the reset-password endpoint along with the new password. If the account requires email verification first, the response LoginStatus will indicate this. Authorization: Public endpoint — no authentication required. Side Effects: Sends a password reset email with a 6-digit verification code to the user. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request is null or UserName is missing. The response LoginStatus indicates IsPendingEmailConfirmation (4) if the account needs email verification first, IsPendingNewAccountSignup (5) if the account is not fully registered, or IsDisabled if the account is disabled.

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Account.ForgotPasswordRequestModel containing the UserName (email address) of the account.

200.description: The password reset code was sent, or the response indicates a pending account state that must be resolved first.

400.description: The request is null or UserName is missing.

post.summary: Register a new user account with a unique email address.
post.description: Behavior: Creates a new user account with the specified email, name, and password. The email must be unique. Depending on the tenant configuration, the account may be auto-approved (Normal status) or require admin approval (PendingApproval status). A verification email is sent to the user to confirm their email address. Authorization: Public endpoint — no authentication required. Side Effects: Creates a new user account. Sends a verification email to the user. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request is null or Email is empty. Throws if a user with the same email already exists.

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Account.UserRegistrationRequestModel containing Email, FirstName, LastName, Password, and optional custom Properties.

200.description: The account was successfully created. The user must verify their email before logging in.

400.description: The request is null, Email is empty, or a user with this email already exists.

post.summary: Complete registration for a guest user who was invited via the invite-user endpoint.
post.description: Behavior: Completes the registration process for a user who received a guest invitation. The user must have a pending invitation that has not expired. Sets the user's password, verifies their email address, and activates their account so they can log in. Authorization: Public endpoint — no authentication required. The invitation itself serves as authorization. Side Effects: Activates the guest user account and marks their email as verified. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request is null. Throws if the user does not exist, is not in PendingInvitation status, the invitation has expired, no security records exist, or no guest profile is configured.

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Account.UserRegistrationRequestModel containing Email, Password, FirstName, and LastName for the guest user.

200.description: The guest account was successfully activated. The user can now log in.

400.description: The request is null, the user is not in PendingInvitation status, the invitation has expired, or guest profile is missing.

post.summary: Re-send the email verification confirmation code to the user.
post.description: Behavior: Re-sends the email verification confirmation code to the specified user's email address. Use this when the original verification email was not received or the code has expired. Authorization: Public endpoint — no authentication required. Side Effects: Sends a verification email to the user with a new confirmation code. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request is null or UserName is missing. Throws if the user does not exist.

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Account.ResendConfirmationCodeRequestModel containing the UserName (email address) to re-send the code to.

200.description: The confirmation code was re-sent to the user's email address.

400.description: The request is null or UserName is missing.

post.summary: Reset the user's password using a verification code from the forgot-password email.
post.description: Behavior: Completes the password reset flow by validating the verification code from the forgot-password email and setting the new password. For accounts pending approval, this also completes the email verification step. Authorization: Public endpoint — no authentication required. The reset token serves as the credential. Side Effects: Updates the user's password. For pending-approval accounts, also verifies the email address. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request is null or UserName is missing. Throws if the user is disabled or the reset token is invalid/expired.

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Account.ResetPasswordRequestModel containing UserName (email), the Token from the reset email, and the NewPassword to set.

200.description: The password was successfully reset. The user can now log in with the new password.

400.description: The request is null, UserName is missing, the reset token is invalid or expired, or the user is disabled.

get.summary: Get the profile of the currently authenticated user.
get.description: Behavior: Retrieves the profile information for the currently authenticated user based on their access token. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token ([Authorize]). Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 404 if the user profile cannot be found. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

200.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile.

404.description: The user entity was not found for the authenticated user's ID.

post.summary: Verify a user's email address using a confirmation code.
post.description: Behavior: Confirms the user's email address using the verification code sent during registration. If the account requires admin approval, the response LoginStatus will be IsDisabled to indicate the account is verified but not yet approved for use. Authorization: Public endpoint — no authentication required. Side Effects: Marks the user's email address as verified. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request is null or UserName is missing. Throws if the verification token is invalid or expired.

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Account.VerifyEmailRequestModel containing the UserName (email) and the Token from the verification email.

200.description: The email was verified. Check LoginStatus — null means active, IsDisabled means pending approval.

400.description: The request is null, UserName is missing, or the verification token is invalid/expired.

get.summary: Retrieves the VMAP (Video Multiple Ad Playlist) XML for an asset.
get.description: Behavior: Generates and returns the VMAP XML document that defines all ad break positions and their associated VAST ad tag URLs for the specified asset. Video players use this to schedule ad insertions during playback. Authorization: Public endpoint — no authentication required. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 404 if no VMAP data exists for the asset.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to retrieve VMAP data for.

200.description: The VMAP XML was generated successfully.

404.description: No VMAP data found for the asset.

get.summary: Retrieves a VAST (Video Ad Serving Template) XML response for ad playback.
get.description: Behavior: Returns a VAST XML document containing the ad creative details for playback at the specified timecode position. The response can be filtered by content categories and ad position type (pre-roll, mid-roll, post-roll). Authorization: Public endpoint — no authentication required. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 404 if no VAST ad is available for the given parameters.

parameters.name: timeCode
parameters.description: The timecode position in the video where the ad will be inserted.

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description: Optional content category filters for ad targeting.

parameters.name: vpos
parameters.description: The ad position type (e.g., "preroll", "midroll", "postroll").

200.description: The VAST XML was generated successfully.

404.description: No VAST ad available for the given parameters.

get.summary: Records an ad impression tracking beacon.
get.description: Behavior: Accepts an impression beacon request triggered when an ad is displayed to a viewer. Returns a minimal HTML response suitable for use as a tracking pixel. Currently acts as a placeholder for future impression analytics. Authorization: Public endpoint — no authentication required. Side Effects: None currently. Reserved for future impression tracking analytics. Error Scenarios: None — always returns a successful response.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the ad asset that was displayed.

200.description: The impression was recorded.

head.summary: Records an ad impression tracking beacon.
head.description: Behavior: Accepts an impression beacon request triggered when an ad is displayed to a viewer. Returns a minimal HTML response suitable for use as a tracking pixel. Currently acts as a placeholder for future impression analytics. Authorization: Public endpoint — no authentication required. Side Effects: None currently. Reserved for future impression tracking analytics. Error Scenarios: None — always returns a successful response.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the ad asset that was displayed.

200.description: The impression was recorded.

get.summary: Retrieves child folder nodes for a given parent folder in the asset tree.
get.description: Behavior: Returns a paginated, sortable list of child folders under the specified parent. If the parent is the root (empty GUID), all top-level storage bucket folders are returned. Results include folder metadata such as media type, content length, and modification date. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The ID of the parent folder. Use an empty GUID to retrieve top-level folders.

parameters.name: sortColumn
parameters.description: The column to sort by (e.g., "name", "contentLength", "mediaType"). Defaults to "name".

parameters.name: isDesc
parameters.description: Whether to sort in descending order. Defaults to false (ascending).

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: Zero-based page index for pagination. Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of items per page. Defaults to 200.

200.description: The child folders were retrieved successfully.

get.summary: Retrieves the word-level transcription data for an asset.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the detailed transcription with individual word timings and confidence scores for the specified asset. The asset must have been previously transcribed. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if the asset has no transcription data.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the asset to retrieve transcription words for.

200.description: The transcription words were retrieved successfully.

404.description: No transcription data found for the asset.

post.summary: Executes a batch action on one or more assets.
post.description: Behavior: Performs the specified batch action (e.g., delete, archive, restore, reprocess) on the assets identified in the request payload. The action name is specified in the URL path and must correspond to a valid batch action. Results include the status of each affected asset. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Varies by action — may delete assets, start processing jobs, move files, or trigger other asynchronous operations. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the action name is not valid or the request body is missing. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The batch action to perform (e.g., "delete", "archive", "restore", "reprocess"). Case-insensitive.

requestBody.description: The batch payload containing target asset IDs and optional action arguments.

200.description: The batch action was executed successfully.

400.description: The action name is invalid or the request body is missing.

post.summary: Registers an existing storage object as a managed asset.
post.description: Behavior: Looks up an object in storage by its bucket name and object key, then creates a managed asset record for it. The asset will be indexed and made available for browsing, searching, and media operations. Both the bucket name and object key are required. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Creates a new asset record and triggers indexing. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if bucket name or object key is missing. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if no object exists at the specified bucket/key location.

requestBody.description: The registration details including bucket name and object key.

200.description: The asset was registered successfully.

400.description: Bucket name or object key is missing.

404.description: No object found at the specified storage location.

get.summary: Retrieves an asset by its unique identifier.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the asset metadata for the specified ID, including properties such as name, type, status, and storage location. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if no asset exists with the specified ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to retrieve.

200.description: The asset was retrieved successfully.

404.description: No asset found with the specified ID.

delete.summary: Deletes an asset by its unique identifier.
delete.description: Behavior: Permanently deletes the specified asset. If the asset is a folder, all child assets within the folder are also deleted. The asset is removed from storage and the search index. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The asset and its associated files are permanently removed. For folders, all child assets are recursively deleted. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if no asset exists with the specified ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to delete.

200.description: The asset was deleted successfully.

404.description: No asset found with the specified ID.

patch.summary: Updates specific properties of an asset such as its display name or custom metadata.
patch.description: Behavior: Updates the specified properties on the asset without affecting other attributes. Use this for lightweight property changes. For more complex operations (e.g., clipping, language change, archiving), use the dedicated endpoints. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The asset's search index entry is updated with the new properties. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is missing. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to update.

requestBody.description: The properties to update on the asset.

200.description: The asset properties were updated successfully.

400.description: The request body is missing or invalid.

get.summary: Retrieves an asset by its storage bucket name and object key.
get.description: Behavior: Looks up the asset using the storage bucket name and object key combination and returns its metadata. Both parameters are required. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if bucket name or object key is missing. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if no asset exists at the specified storage location.

parameters.name: bucketName
parameters.description: The name of the storage bucket containing the asset. Required.

parameters.name: objectKey
parameters.description: The object key (path) of the asset within the bucket. Required.

200.description: The asset was retrieved successfully.

400.description: Bucket name or object key is missing.

404.description: No asset found at the specified storage location.

post.summary: Changes the language of an asset and triggers AI reprocessing.
post.description: Behavior: Updates the language setting for the asset. This triggers reprocessing of AI-powered features such as transcription, subtitle generation, and text detection to use the new language. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Triggers asynchronous reprocessing of AI data (transcription, subtitles, etc.) for the new language. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is missing. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to update.

requestBody.description: The new language setting for the asset.

200.description: The asset language was updated and reprocessing was triggered.

400.description: The request body is missing or invalid.

post.summary: Creates a new folder asset under the specified parent folder.
post.description: Behavior: Creates a new folder within the specified parent folder. The folder display name is required and must be provided in the request body. The new folder is immediately available for browsing and uploading assets into. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: A new folder is created in storage and indexed for search. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the display name is missing or the request body is invalid. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: parentId
parameters.description: The ID of the parent folder where the new folder will be created.

requestBody.description: The new folder details including the display name.

200.description: The folder was successfully created.

400.description: The folder was not created — display name is missing or invalid.

post.summary: Creates a placeholder asset under the specified parent folder.
post.description: Behavior: Creates a new placeholder asset within the specified parent folder. A placeholder reserves a spot in the asset tree before actual media content is uploaded. The asset name is required. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: A new placeholder asset record is created and indexed. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the asset name is missing or the request body is invalid. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: parentId
parameters.description: The ID of the parent folder where the placeholder will be created.

requestBody.description: The placeholder details including the asset name.

200.description: The placeholder was successfully created.

400.description: The placeholder was not created — asset name is missing or invalid.

post.summary: Creates a duplicate copy of the specified asset.
post.description: Behavior: Creates a full copy of the asset including its metadata and media files. The duplicated asset is placed in the same folder as the original. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: A new asset is created with copied files and metadata. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if no asset exists with the specified ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to duplicate.

200.description: The asset was duplicated successfully.

404.description: No asset found with the specified ID.

post.summary: Copies an asset to a different location.
post.description: Behavior: Creates a copy of the specified asset in the target location provided in the batch model. The original asset remains unchanged. The batch model should include the destination folder ID in the action arguments. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: A new asset copy is created at the target location. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is missing. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if no asset exists with the specified ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to copy.

requestBody.description: The batch payload containing the target destination and optional arguments.

200.description: The asset was copied successfully.

400.description: The request body is missing or invalid.

404.description: No asset found with the specified ID.

post.summary: Archives an asset to long-term storage.
post.description: Behavior: Moves the asset to archival storage, reducing storage costs while preserving the asset for future retrieval. Archived assets are not immediately accessible for streaming or download — they must first be restored using the restore endpoint. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token with file write permissions on the asset. Side Effects: The asset's storage class is changed to archival. The asset will become unavailable for direct access until restored. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 403 if the caller does not have write permissions on the asset.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to archive.

200.description: The asset was archived successfully.

403.description: Caller does not have write permissions on the asset.

post.summary: Triggers re-indexing of an asset in the search index.
post.description: Behavior: Queues the asset for re-indexing, which refreshes its metadata in the search index. Use this after external changes to an asset that may not have been automatically detected. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The asset is queued for asynchronous re-indexing. The search index will be updated once processing completes. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to re-index.

200.description: The asset was queued for re-indexing.

post.summary: Moves an asset to a different folder.
post.description: Behavior: Moves the specified asset to the target folder provided in the batch model. The asset retains its identity and metadata but is relocated in the folder hierarchy. The batch model should include the destination folder ID in the action arguments. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The asset is relocated to the new folder. The search index is updated to reflect the new location. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is missing. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to move.

requestBody.description: The batch payload containing the destination folder and optional arguments.

200.description: The asset was moved successfully.

400.description: The request body is missing or invalid.

get.summary: Retrieves the full parent folder hierarchy for an asset.
get.description: Behavior: Returns all ancestor folders for the specified asset, from the root down to the immediate parent. Results are structured hierarchically — each folder's identifiers object includes a children attribute pointing to the next sub-folder in the chain. The specified asset itself is not included in the results. Works for both file and folder assets. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the asset to retrieve parent folders for. Can be either a file or folder.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of folders to retrieve per level of the tree. Defaults to 100.

200.description: The parent folder hierarchy was retrieved successfully.

get.summary: Retrieves the list of uploads for the current user.
get.description: Behavior: Returns all uploads initiated by the current user. By default, only in-progress uploads are returned. Set includeCompletedUploads to true to also include completed uploads. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: includeCompletedUploads
parameters.description: If true, includes completed uploads in the results. If false, only in-progress uploads are returned.

200.description: The upload list was retrieved successfully.

get.summary: Retrieves the individual upload parts for a multipart upload.
get.description: Behavior: Returns all parts associated with the specified upload, including both completed and pending parts. Each part includes its upload URL, part number, and completion status. Use this to track the progress of a multipart upload or to resume a partially completed upload. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the asset upload to retrieve parts for.

200.description: The upload parts were retrieved successfully.

get.summary: Retrieves detailed information for a specific segment of an asset.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the full detail model for the specified segment, including media properties, URLs, metadata, and processing status. Segments represent individual clips or sections within a parent asset. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if the asset or segment is not found.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the parent asset.

parameters.name: segmentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the segment within the asset.

200.description: The segment detail was retrieved successfully.

404.description: The asset or segment was not found.

get.summary: Retrieves detailed information for a specific screenshot within an asset segment.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the full detail model for the specified screenshot, including image properties, URLs, and metadata. Screenshots are images captured at specific timecodes within a video asset segment. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if the asset, segment, or screenshot is not found.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the parent asset.

parameters.name: segmentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the segment within the asset.

parameters.name: screenshotId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the screenshot.

200.description: The screenshot detail was retrieved successfully.

404.description: The asset, segment, or screenshot was not found.

post.summary: Creates a clip (sub-segment) from a video asset.
post.description: Behavior: Extracts a segment from the specified video asset based on the provided timecode range. The clip is created as a new related asset linked to the original. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: A new clip asset is created asynchronously. The clip will appear as a related asset once processing completes. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is missing or invalid. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if the asset is not found.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the video asset to clip.

requestBody.description: The clip parameters including start and end timecodes.

200.description: The clip operation was initiated successfully.

400.description: The request body is missing or invalid.

404.description: The asset was not found.

post.summary: Captures a screenshot from a video asset at a specific timecode.
post.description: Behavior: Generates a still image from the video at the timecode specified in the request. The screenshot is saved as a related asset linked to the original video. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: A new screenshot image asset is created asynchronously. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is missing or invalid. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if the asset is not found.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the video asset to capture a screenshot from.

requestBody.description: The screenshot parameters including the timecode.

200.description: The screenshot operation was initiated successfully.

400.description: The request body is missing or invalid.

404.description: The asset was not found.

get.summary: Retrieves secure streaming cookies for an asset's related media.
get.description: Behavior: Generates signed cookies required for secure streaming of a related asset (e.g., proxy video, preview). The response includes cookies both in the response headers (for browser-based playback) and in the response body (for programmatic access). Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Signed streaming cookies are set in the HTTP response headers. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if the asset or related asset is not found.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the parent asset.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the related asset to generate streaming cookies for.

200.description: The streaming cookies were generated successfully.

404.description: The asset or related asset was not found.

get.summary: Retrieves the metadata summary (labels) for an asset or folder.

get.summary: Lists deleted files and virtual folders within an S3 bucket at a specific prefix level.
get.description: Behavior: Scans S3 object versions for items whose latest version is a delete marker, then groups the results into a folder/file hierarchy. Items whose key has additional path segments beyond `prefix` are collapsed into a virtual folder row; items directly under `prefix` are returned as individual file rows. Folders appear before files, both groups sorted alphabetically. Pagination: Cursor-based. Pass the integer value from `ListResultModel.NextPageOffset` as `pageOffset` to retrieve the next page. Folder child counts: Accurate up to 1 000 items; beyond that, `ChildCountExceeded` is set to `true` and `ChildCount` is 1 000. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Caller must be a System Administrator. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if `bucketName` is missing. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 403 if the caller is not a System Administrator.

parameters.name: BucketName
parameters.description: Gets or sets the S3 bucket to scan for deleted items. Required.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description:
Gets or sets the S3 key prefix to scope the scan to a specific folder.
Use an empty string or null to scan from the bucket root.
When navigating into a virtual folder, pass its full prefix (e.g. "Content/Videos/").

parameters.name: MinDeletedDate
parameters.description:
Gets or sets the optional minimum delete date filter. Only items whose S3 delete
marker was placed on or after this date will be returned.

parameters.name: PageSize
parameters.description:
Gets or sets the maximum number of top-level rows (folders + files) to return per page.
Defaults to 200.

parameters.name: PageOffset
parameters.description:
Gets or sets the zero-based page offset for cursor-based pagination.
Pass the value returned in `ListResultModel.NextPageOffset` to fetch the next page.
Defaults to 0.

200.description: The deleted items were retrieved successfully.

400.description: The bucket name is missing or the request is otherwise invalid.

403.description: Caller is not a System Administrator.

post.summary: Undeletes a single deleted file or all deleted files under a folder prefix.
post.description: Behavior: Removes the S3 delete marker(s) so that the previous version of each object becomes the latest version again. Once the marker is removed, the existing S3-event notification pipeline automatically re-registers each file in DynamoDB and Elasticsearch — no manual catalog repair is required. Single-file mode: Set `isFolder` to `false` and supply both `objectKey` and `versionId` (the delete-marker version ID returned by the list endpoint). Only that specific marker is removed. Folder mode: Set `isFolder` to `true` and supply `objectKey` as the folder prefix. Every delete marker whose key starts with that prefix is removed. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Caller must be a System Administrator. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if required fields are missing. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 403 if the caller is not a System Administrator.

requestBody.description: The undelete request.

200.description: The item(s) were undeleted successfully.

400.description: Required fields are missing or invalid.

403.description: Caller is not a System Administrator.

get.summary: Returns the full S3 version history for a single file, newest version first.
get.description: Behavior: Lists every S3 version (including delete markers) for the asset. The bucket name and object key are resolved from the asset record. Each entry includes the version ID, size, date, storage class, and — when correlated within a 5-minute window against the audit history table — the user who created that version. Pagination: Cursor-based. Pass the integer value from `ListResultModel.NextPageOffset` as `pageOffset` to retrieve the next page. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Caller must be a System Administrator. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the asset ID is missing or the asset does not exist. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 403 if the caller is not a System Administrator.

parameters.name: AssetId
parameters.description:
Gets or sets the ID of the asset whose version history is requested. Required.
The bucket name and object key are resolved from the asset record.

parameters.name: PageSize
parameters.description: Gets or sets the maximum number of versions to return per page. Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: PageOffset
parameters.description:
Gets or sets the zero-based row offset for cursor-based pagination.
Pass the value returned in `NextPageOffset` to retrieve the following page.

200.description: The version history was retrieved successfully.

400.description: The asset ID is missing or the asset was not found.

403.description: Caller is not a System Administrator.

post.summary: Reverts an S3 object to a specific prior version by performing a server-side copy.
post.description: Behavior: Creates a new latest version of the object whose content is identical to the specified historical version. The bucket name and object key are resolved from the asset record. No data is downloaded or uploaded — the operation is entirely server-side. Once the new version is created, the existing S3-event notification pipeline automatically re-registers the asset in DynamoDB and Elasticsearch. Delete markers: Delete markers cannot be reverted to using this endpoint. To undelete a file whose current version is a delete marker, use `POST /admin/asset/history/undelete` instead. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Caller must be a System Administrator. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if required fields are missing, the asset was not found, or the specified version does not exist. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 403 if the caller is not a System Administrator.
